responses: Hello! Welcome to the GNDEC College Helpdesk Chatbot. I am here to help you with college-related information such as admissions, fee structure, timetables, departments, hostel facilities, exams, scholarships, placements, and important official links. Please let me know what you would like to know.

Hi! It’s great to have you here. I can assist you with many college queries including admission process, fee details, class timetables, exam datesheets, department information, hostel details, and student services. Please type your question and I will do my best to guide you.
Hey! I’m your college helpdesk assistant. You can ask me about admissions, academic calendar, syllabus, exam portal, department contacts, hostel information, and much more. How may I help you today?
Goodbye! I hope I was able to help you. If you need any more information related to the college, admissions, fees, hostel, exams, or departments, feel free to return anytime.
Bye! Thank you for using the GNDEC College Helpdesk Chatbot. Whenever you need guidance about college-related matters, I will be here to help you.
See you later! If you have any more questions about your college, timetable, notices, admission process, or official links, you can always come back and ask.
You’re most welcome! I’m glad I could help you. If you want, you can also ask me about admissions, fee structure, timetables, hostel, exams, scholarships, or placements.
Happy to help! If you have any other college-related questions, feel free to ask anytime.
It’s my pleasure to assist you. Please let me know if you need help with any other college information.

I can help you with many types of college-related information. For example, you can ask me about admission process, eligibility, fee structure, hostel details, scholarship information, class timetables, academic calendar, exam datesheet, syllabus, department details, placement information, and important official links. Please ask your question in simple words and I will guide you.

I’m designed to assist students, parents, and visitors with common college queries. You can ask me about admissions, courses, departments, exam portal, result guidance, fee payment, hostel, transport, scholarships, notices, and certificates. If you are not sure what to ask, simply type a topic like 'admission process' or 'CSE timetable'.

I can provide guidance related to college admissions, fees, academic resources, hostels, departments, placements, student services, and official portals. You may also ask for links to important pages such as timetable, admission portal, exam portal, or downloads section.

The full name of the college is Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College, commonly known as GNDEC. It is a well-known engineering institution and you can find more official information on the college website.
This college is Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College, also called GNDEC. For official details, departments, academic notices, and student resources, you may visit the main website.
The college name is Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College. If you want, I can also help you with official website link, departments, courses, or admission information.

Sure! You can visit the official GNDEC website for authentic and updated information related to academics, departments, notices, student services, and administration. Please use the official link provided below.
Of course! The official college website is the best place to check notices, admissions, academics, departments, and important updates. Please open the official website link below.
Here is the official college website. It is recommended to use this source whenever you need the latest and most accurate information about the college.

The fee structure for Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC), Ludhiana for the 2025–2026 session varies by program, with typical first-year academic fees ranging from approximately ₹74,539 to ₹1.21 Lakhs.

Estimated Total Academic Fees (2025–2026)
The total fee for complete course durations is generally as follows:
Course 	Total Academic Fees (Approx.)	Duration
B.Tech	₹4.49 Lakhs	4 Years
B.Tech (Lateral Entry)	₹3.32 Lakhs	3 Years
B.Arch	₹3.00 Lakhs - ₹4.42 Lakhs	5 Years
BCA / BBA	₹2.09 Lakhs	3 Years
B.Com	₹2.30 Lakhs	3 Years
MBA / MCA	₹2.06 Lakhs	2 Years
M.Tech	₹1.62 Lakhs	2 Years

Semester Fee Details (January – June 2026 Session)
According to the latest college notices, semester fees for ongoing batches (e.g., Batch 2025-26) are deposited through the college's Academic Portal.
BCA/BBA/B.Voc (Day Scholars): Approximately ₹33,650 for General/Boys and ₹33,650 for General/Girls.
SC/ST (Eligible) Students: Fees can be significantly lower, starting around ₹5,000 to ₹8,000 per semester.
Refundable Security: A security fee (approx. ₹4,000 for academic and ₹2,000 for hostel) is usually payable at the time of admission.

Hostel and Mess Fees
Hostel charges are separate and typically paid per semester:
Hostel Fee: Approximately ₹11,113 to ₹19,300 per semester depending on gender and room type.
Mess Fee: Estimated at ₹15,200 per semester.

Payment Methods
Fees are accepted through the following modes:
Online: Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, and UPI via the GNDEC Payment Gateway.
Offline: NEFT/RTGS or Demand Draft (DD) in favour of "Principal, Guru Nanak Dev Engg. College, Ludhiana" payable at Punjab and Sind Bank, Gill Branch. Cheques are not accepted.

The fee structure at Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC), Ludhiana, for the 2026-27 session varies by course and category, with the total academic fee for the flagship B.Tech program being approximately ₹4.49 Lakhs.

Academic Fee Structure (General Category)
Fees are typically collected on a per-semester basis. Odd semesters (1st, 3rd, etc.) often include higher one-time charges compared to even semesters.
Course 	1st Year Fee (Approx.)	Total Course Fee (Approx.)
B.Tech (CSE, IT, ECE, Civil, ME, EE)	₹1,24,639	₹4.49 Lakhs
B.Tech Lateral Entry (2nd Year Entry)	₹1,24,639	₹3.32 Lakhs - ₹3.39 Lakhs
B.Architecture (B.Arch)	₹1,01,539	₹4.42 Lakhs
MBA / MCA	₹1,14,739	₹2.06 Lakhs
BBA / BCA	₹70,239	₹2.09 Lakhs
B.Com	₹48,500	₹2.3 Lakhs
M.Tech	₹1,10,624	₹1.62 Lakhs

Hostel & Additional Charges
Living on campus involves separate costs for accommodation and meals.
Hostel Rent: Approximately ₹14,272 per semester for boys and ₹11,908 for girls.
Mess Charges: Roughly ₹15,200 per semester.
Security Deposits: A refundable mess security of ₹7,000 is usually required at the time of admission.
Total Annual Cost (with Hostel): Students should budget between ₹1.5 Lakhs and ₹1.8 Lakhs per year including academic fees.

Scholarships & Fee Concessions
GNDEC provides several avenues to reduce financial burden based on category or merit:
Post Matric Scholarship (PMS): SC/ST candidates from Punjab with eligible family income can have their fees significantly reduced, sometimes to as low as ₹10,000–₹12,500 per semester.
Tuition Fee Waiver (TFW): Eligible Punjab residents (based on family income) may pay approximately ₹11,450–₹17,190 per semester.
Sikh Minority Quota: Candidates who qualify for the Sikh Religion Exam (SRE) are eligible for seats reserved specifically for the community.
Alumni & Merit Scholarships: Various batches and trusts provide annual aid ranging from ₹10,000 to full fee coverage for needy and meritorious students.

Fees are primarily deposited through the GNDEC Academic Portal using online modes like NEFT, UPI, or Credit/Debit cards.

If you want GNDEC fees, you can also check course-wise and semester-wise fee details along with hostel and payment information. Fee structure can vary by program, category, and session, so students usually see academic fees, refundable security, hostel fees, mess charges, and online/offline payment options separately. You can also ask specifically for B.Tech fees, hostel fees, semester fee details, or scholarship-related fee concessions.

Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) is located on Gill Road (Ludhiana-Malerkotla State Highway) in the Gill Park area of Ludhiana, Punjab.

Key Location Details
Address: Gill Road, Gill Park, Ludhiana, Punjab 141006, India.
Campus Size: The institution is situated on a sprawling 86 to 88-acre campus.
Major Landmark: The college is located right next to the Gill Canal (Sidhwan Canal).

Proximity to Transit Hubs
Ludhiana Railway Station: Approximately 6 to 8 km away.
Amar Shaheed Sukhdev Interstate Bus Terminal: Roughly 5 to 6 km away.
Ludhiana Airport (Sahnewal): About 15 km away.

How to Reach
The college is well-connected by public and private transport. For those coming from outside the city, Ludhiana is roughly 90 km from Chandigarh and 300 km from New Delhi.

GNDEC is situated on Gill Road in Ludhiana, Punjab, in the Gill Park area. It is known for its large campus and is located near the Gill Canal, also called the Sidhwan Canal. Students and visitors can reach it easily from Ludhiana railway station, bus terminal, and Sahnewal airport.

Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) in Ludhiana has opened admissions for the 2026-27 session for various undergraduate and postgraduate programs. The college has recently established a new dedicated Admission Cell to handle the process.

How to Apply
The primary application process is online through the official portal:
Official Admission Portal: admission.gndec.ac.in.
Counseling Fees: A non-refundable processing fee of ₹2,000 is typically required for centralized online counseling. For some direct college-level applications, a fee of ₹1,200 (₹200 registration + ₹1,000 processing) may apply.
Key Steps:
Register on the GNDEC Admission Portal.
Fill in personal and academic details.
Upload required documents and "Lock" the form.
Generate a File Number to ensure the application is considered.

Course Eligibility and Selection
Admissions are merit-based, either through national/state-level exams or qualifying exam marks.
Course 	Entrance Exam(s)	Eligibility Highlights
B.Tech	JEE Main	10+2 with Physics & Maths (Min. 45% for General)
B.Arch	NATA	10+2 with 50% aggregate
MBA	CMAT	Bachelor's Degree with 50%
MCA	PTU MCA / Merit	Bachelor's with Maths at 10+2 or Graduation
M.Tech	GATE	B.Tech in relevant field with 50%
BBA / BCA / B.Com	Qualifying Merit	10+2 in any stream (Specific marks for B.Com)

Admission Categories & Counseling
Seat Allocation: 85% of seats are reserved for Punjab State candidates, and 15% are open to Other States.
Counseling Rounds:
IKGPTU Centralized Counseling: Initial rounds are conducted online by I.K. Gujral Punjab Technical University.
Direct/Spot Counseling: Vacant seats after centralized rounds are filled directly by GNDEC at the college auditorium.
Sikh Minority Quota: Candidates must qualify for the Sikh Religion Exam (SRE) to claim seats under this quota.

Helpdesk & Contacts
For specific queries, you can contact the admission helpline numbers provided by the college:
B.Tech / M.Tech: 9041495448, 8968553073
MBA / BBA: 9876156364, 9417992553
BCA / MCA: 9876700810, 9417271184
WhatsApp Helpline: 7347200448

If you want admission information for GNDEC, the college is taking applications for the 2026-27 session for different UG and PG programs. The application process is mainly online through the admission portal, where students register, fill in details, upload documents, lock the form, and generate a file number. Admission may happen through centralized counseling as well as direct or spot counseling for vacant seats.

To be eligible for admission at Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) for the 2026-27 session, you must meet both general academic requirements and specific entrance exam criteria.

Undergraduate (UG) Programs
B.Tech: 10+2 with Physics, Math, and a science elective (min. 45% Gen/40% SC/ST) + JEE Main score.
B.Arch: 10+2 with Math (min. 50% aggregate) + valid NATA score.
BBA/BCA: 10+2 (min. 50% Gen/45% reserved) based on merit.
Lateral Entry: 3-year Diploma/B.Sc. (min. 45% Gen/40% SC/ST).

Postgraduate (PG) Programs
MBA/MCA/M.Tech: Relevant bachelor's degree with 50% marks (45% for SC/ST), with MBA requiring CMAT and M.Tech preferring GATE qualifications.

Quota & Other Specifics
Sikh Minority: 50% seats, requires passing the SRE with 40%.
Rural: Requires a certificate and proof of 5 years of rural schooling.
Residency: 85% seats for Punjab residents, 15% for others.

Eligibility at GNDEC depends on the program you want to join. For engineering courses like B.Tech, students usually need 10+2 with Physics and Maths along with JEE Main. For B.Arch, NATA is required. For MBA, MCA, and M.Tech, graduation-based eligibility applies with specific marks and in some cases entrance exams. There are also category and quota conditions such as Sikh Minority, Rural, and Punjab residency rules.

Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) offers a wide array of courses for the 2026-27 session, ranging from undergraduate engineering to doctoral research programs.

Undergraduate (UG) Courses
These programs typically have a duration of 3 to 5 years.
B.Tech (Bachelor of Technology): Available in disciplines such as:
Computer Science & Engineering
Information Technology
Civil Engineering
Mechanical Engineering
Electrical Engineering
Electronics & Communication Engineering
Robotics and Artificial Intelligence
Production Engineering
Other UG Degrees:
B.Arch (Bachelor of Architecture)
BBA (Bachelor of Business Administration)
BCA (Bachelor of Computer Applications)
B.Com (Bachelor of Commerce)
B.Voc (Interior Design)

Postgraduate (PG) Courses
Most PG programs are 2-year full-time degrees.
M.Tech (Master of Technology): Offered in full-time and part-time modes with specialisations including:
Structural Engineering
Geo-Technical Engineering
Environmental Engineering
Power Engineering
Computer Science and Information Technology
Management & Computer Applications:
MBA (Master of Business Administration)
MCA (Master of Computer Applications)
M.Sc: Programs in Physics and Chemistry.

Diploma & Doctoral Programs
Post Graduate Diplomas: 1-year courses in fields like VLSI Design, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, Data Science, and Industrial Safety.
Ph.D.: Doctoral research is available in major engineering departments like Civil, Electrical, and Mechanical Engineering.

For more specific information or course brochures, you can visit the GNDEC Academics Page.

If you want to know GNDEC courses, the college offers UG, PG, diploma, and doctoral-level programs. Major areas include engineering, architecture, management, computer applications, commerce, science, and vocational courses. Students can choose from regular B.Tech branches, M.Tech specialisations, MBA, MCA, M.Sc, and research options depending on their eligibility and interest.

For the 2026-27 session, Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) offers seven primary B.Tech specialisations. Total seat intake for regular 4-year programs is approximately 960 seats.

B.Tech Specialisations & Seat Intake
Branch 	Sanctioned Intake
Computer Science and Engineering	300
Information Technology	180
Electrical Engineering	120
Civil Engineering	120
Mechanical Engineering	120
Electronics & Communication Engineering	90
Robotics and Artificial Intelligence	30

Additional B.Tech Options
B.Tech Lateral Entry: For diploma or B.Sc. holders, direct admission to the 3rd semester (2nd year) is available across these branches.
B.Tech for Working Professionals: Lateral entry programs specifically designed for working professionals are offered in Civil Engineering and Mechanical Engineering with an intake of 30 seats each.
Honours & Minor Degrees: Students can pursue additional Minor or Honour degrees in specialised areas alongside their primary B.Tech major, aligned with the new National Education Policy (NEP) curriculum.

Key Highlights for 2026 Admissions
Entrance Exam: Admission is primarily based on JEE Main scores through IKGPTU centralized online counselling.
Fees: The total tuition fee for most 4-year B.Tech programs is approximately ₹4.49 Lakhs.
Reservation: 85% of seats are reserved for Punjab residents, and 15% are open to Other State.

GNDEC B.Tech courses include major engineering branches like CSE, IT, EE, Civil, Mechanical, ECE, and Robotics and Artificial Intelligence. The college also provides lateral entry options and some working professional pathways. Students may also get opportunities for honours and minor degrees under the NEP structure.

The Department of Computer Science and Engineering (CSE) at Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) is currently led by Dr. Kiran Jyoti.

HOD Profile: Dr. Kiran Jyoti
Designation: Professor and Head of Department.
Academic Qualification:
Ph.D. (CSE) from Shri JJT University, Rajasthan (2013).
M.Tech. (CSE) from GNDEC, Ludhiana (2007).
B.Tech. (CSE) from NIT Jalandhar (2000).
Experience: She has over 25 years of teaching experience at GNDEC, having served in various capacities since 2000. She was promoted to Professor and Head in August 2025.
Research: Her research interests include roughly 60 publications in reputed journals.

Contact Information
Email: kiranjyotibains@gndec.ac.in or cse@gndec.ac.in.
Phone: 0161-5064547 (Direct Office) or 0161-5064501 (Ext. 5064501).
Location: CSE Block, Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College, Ludhiana.

Departmental Leadership Team
In addition to the HOD, several senior professors hold key administrative roles within the department and college:
Dr. Parminder Singh: Professor (CSE) and Dean of Student Welfare.
Dr. Akshay Girdhar: Professor (IT) and Dean of Academics.
Dr. Jasbir Singh Saini: Associate Professor and HOD of Computer Applications (MCA).

If you are asking about the CSE HOD at GNDEC, the department is headed by Dr. Kiran Jyoti. Her academic background includes B.Tech from NIT Jalandhar, M.Tech from GNDEC, and Ph.D. in CSE. She has long teaching experience and holds the role of Professor and Head.

Official Exam & Results Portal
The primary portal for current students to access exam forms, date sheets, and result statuses is the GNDEC Exam Portal.
Admit Cards & Fee Slips: Active students can download their admit cards for end-semester examinations and generate semester fee slips via the GNDEC Academic Portal.
Results: You can view result notifications and download result gazettes directly through the Official Results Page.

If you want the GNDEC exam portal, students generally use it for exam forms, datesheets, and result status. Admit cards and semester fee slips are usually handled through the academic portal, while result notifications and gazettes are available through the results page.

Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) provides residential facilities for approximately 1,200 students across four distinct hostels, each assigned to specific academic years or programs to maintain a structured environment.

Hostel Assignments
Rooms are typically allocated based on your year of study:
Hostel No. 1 (Boys): Generally for M.Tech (1st Year) students.
Hostel No. 2 (Boys): Assigned to B.Tech (2nd Year) and Lateral Entry (LEET) students.
Hostel No. 5 (Boys): Dedicated exclusively to B.Tech (1st Year) students to ensure a supportive start.
Hostel No. 4 (Girls): A common hostel for female students from all streams.

Facilities & Amenities
The hostels offer essential services to support student life:
Room Features: Rooms typically accommodate 3–4 students in the first two years, with options for single rooms available for seniors (3rd year onwards).
Standard Furniture: Each student is provided with a bed, almirah (wardrobe), study table, chair, and a fan.
General Utilities: Hostels are equipped with geysers for hot water, washing machines, water purifiers, and 24/7 power backup.
Recreation: Every hostel features a common room with dish TV, indoor games (Chess, Badminton, Table Tennis), and newspapers.
Connectivity: High-speed Wi-Fi is available throughout all hostel premises.
Safety & Medical: The campus is monitored by CCTV cameras, and an ambulance is available on standby for emergencies.

Mess & Dining
Dining Halls: Each hostel has its own air-cooled and spacious dining hall.
Meal Quality: Reviews are mixed; while some students find the food standard and hygienic, others describe it as average. The menu is varied and typically includes items like paneer and rajma multiple times a week.
Monthly Mess Diet: The cost for meals is approximately ₹2,700–₹3,000 per month.

Hostel Fee Overview (Per Semester)
Fees vary slightly by program, but general estimates for the 2026 session are as follows:
Program 	Boys' Hostel Fee	Girls' Hostel Fee
B.Tech (1st Sem)	₹19,300	₹16,900
B.Tech (Subsequent Semesters)	~₹11,900–₹14,300	~₹11,100–₹11,900
MBA / MCA / M.Tech	~₹11,963–₹16,963	~₹11,963–₹16,963
Security Deposit: A refundable security fee of ₹5,000–₹6,000 is usually payable at the time of initial admission.

If you want a hostel overview of GNDEC, the college provides separate hostels for boys and girls with room allotment based on year and program. Facilities usually include furniture, Wi-Fi, common rooms, water and power support, security, and mess arrangements. Fees depend on program, semester, and hostel category.

The Girls' Hostel (Hostel No. 4) at GNDEC is a common residence for all female students, including those in B.Tech, B.Arch, MBA, MCA, and M.Tech programs. It is designed to provide a secure and comfortable environment within the campus.

Key Features & Facilities
Capacity: Can accommodate approximately 350 students across multiple floors.
Security: High priority is given to safety with 24/7 security guards, CCTV surveillance at entry/exit points, and strict gate timings (usually entry by 7:00 PM - 8:00 PM).
Amenities:
Common Room: Equipped with an LCD TV, indoor games like table tennis and carrom, and daily newspapers.
Internet: Campus-wide high-speed Wi-Fi connectivity.
Convenience: On-site facilities include a water cooler with RO, geysers, a gym, and a cooperative mess system.
Medical: A dispensary with a lady doctor is available for emergencies.

Fee Structure (Per Semester)
The girls' hostel fees are slightly lower than the boys' hostels.
First Semester: Approximately ₹16,900 (includes refundable security).
Subsequent Semesters: Ranges between ₹11,108 and ₹11,908.
Mess Charges: Roughly ₹2,800–₹3,200 per month, paid separately from the hostel rent.

Room Type
Freshmen (1st Year) are typically allotted triple or quadruple sharing rooms.
Senior students (3rd & 4th Year) may opt for double or single occupancy rooms based on availability and merit.

Hostel Administration
Warden: Dr. Puneet Narang (Main warden) and Assistant Wardens manage day-to-day operations and student welfare.
Application: Current students can apply or renew their seats via the GNDEC Hostel Portal.

If you are asking about the girls hostel at GNDEC, Hostel No. 4 is the main hostel for female students from different programs. It focuses on security, comfort, and campus facilities, and includes mess, Wi-Fi, common room, medical support, and different room-sharing options depending on year and availability.

Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) provides three dedicated hostels for boys, accommodating roughly 850 outstation students. Each hostel is assigned based on the student's year and course of study to ensure a disciplined environment.

Hostel Assignments
Hostel 	Applicable Students	Primary Contact
Hostel No. 1	M.Tech First Year	Mr. Kewal Singh
Hostel No. 2	B.Tech (2nd Year) and Lateral Entry (LEET) students	Mr. Jagmail Singh
Hostel No. 5	B.Tech (1st Year) students	Mr. Gurdev Singh

Fee Structure (Session Jan–June 2026)
Hostel fees are payable per semester via the GNDEC Academic Gateway.
Hostel Rent: Approximately ₹14,272 per semester.
Mess Fee: Fixed at ₹15,200 per semester (approx. ₹2,700–₹3,000 monthly).
One-time/Refundable Charges:
Mess Security: ₹7,000 (Refundable).
Hostel Security: ₹5,000 (Refundable, paid at admission).
Servant & Utensil Fees: Approximately ₹8,000 combined.

Facilities & Room Types
Room Occupancy: First and second-year students typically stay in triple or four-sharing rooms. Single rooms are generally available starting from the 3rd year.
Essential Furniture: Each student receives a bed, almirah (wardrobe), study table, chair, and fan.
Amenities: Hostels are equipped with high-speed Wi-Fi, backup power supply, water purifiers, washing machines, and geysers.
Recreation: Each hostel features a common room with Dish TV, newspapers, and indoor sports like Table Tennis and Chess.
Security & Safety: Monitored by CCTV cameras and 24/7 security personnel. An ambulance is available for medical emergencies.

Important Rules
Attendance: Students must be present for night attendance as per specified timings.
Prohibitions: Ragging, smoking, drinking, and drug use are strictly prohibited.
Visitors: Unauthorized persons are not allowed to stay overnight.

If you want boys hostel details at GNDEC, the college provides multiple dedicated hostels for male students depending on year and course. Hostel fees usually include rent and separate mess charges, and students get facilities like Wi-Fi, furniture, security, common room, utilities, and structured hostel rules.

The Central Library at Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) is a fully computerized, air-conditioned facility that supports the academic and research needs of the institute with a collection of over 97,000 volumes.

Library Timings
Weekdays (Monday to Friday): 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM.
Saturdays: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (referral services only).
Closed: Sundays and Gazetted Holidays.

Key Sections and Facilities
Reading Room: A dedicated space on the ground floor where students can bring their own books for self-study.
Book Bank Section: Provides a complete set of textbooks to SC/ST students for an entire semester.
Welfare Section: Specifically maintains books for financially weaker sections of students.
Digital Resources: Access to over 500,000 e-resources, including peer-reviewed e-journals and e-books from publishers like IEEE, Springer, and Science Direct.
Inter-Library Loan: Connected with DELNET, allowing students to borrow books or documents not available in the central collection.

Membership and Borrowing Rules
Eligibility: Open to all students, faculty, and staff. No membership fee is charged.
B.Tech/PG Entitlement: Students can typically borrow 4 books for a duration of 14 days.
Overdue Fines: A fine of ₹1 per day is charged for books returned after the due date.
Loss of Books: If a book is lost or damaged, the borrower must pay twice the cost of the book or replace it with a new one.

If you are asking about the GNDEC library, it is a large centralized academic library with physical books, digital resources, reading spaces, book bank support, and borrowing facilities for students, faculty, and staff.

Library Timings
Main Library: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM (Monday to Friday).
Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM.
Sunday/Holidays: Closed.
Reading Room: Often remains open for extended hours (until 10:00 PM or later) during final examinations.

If you want the GNDEC library timing, the main library usually runs from morning to evening on weekdays, has shorter timing on Saturday, and remains closed on Sundays and holidays. During final exams, the reading room may remain open for longer hours.

The official notices for Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) are hosted across several specialized portals depending on the subject:

Primary Notice Portals
ERP Noticeboard: erp.gndec.ac.in/notice
This is the main hub for official administrative notices, readmission alerts, and general campus updates.
Examination Portal: exam.gndec.ac.in
Directly contains notices regarding final datesheets, special chance exams, and result announcements.
Admission Notice Section: admission.gndec.ac.in/important_notices.php
Specifically for new applicants regarding spot counselling and document requirements.

Department-Specific Notice Links
You can also find notices tailored to specific engineering branches:
Applied Sciences: appsc.gndec.ac.in/notices
Information Technology: it.gndec.ac.in
Sessional Notices: gndec.ac.in/?q=node/162 (Contains datesheets and syllabi for all departments)

Direct Links to Key Recent Notices
Semester Fee Notice (Jan–Jun 2026): gndec.ac.in/?q=fee_notice
Special Chance Exam Form: erp.gndec.ac.in/noticeboard/examination-form-special-chance-examination
Result Gazette Notifications: academics.gndec.ac.in/resultnotification/

If you want official GNDEC notices, they are not always on a single page. Administrative notices usually appear on the ERP noticeboard, exam-related notices on the exam portal, admission notices on the admission portal, and some branch-specific notices on department websites.

The official holiday list and academic calendar for the 2026 session are available through the following direct links from the GNDEC Ludhiana official website:

Official 2026 PDF Links
List of Holidays 2026 (Full PDF): gndec.ac.in/sites/default/files/LoH26.pdf
Includes all Gazetted and Restricted holidays for the entire calendar year.
Academic Calendar Jan–June 2026: gndec.ac.in/?q=node/23
This page hosts the current semester's academic schedule, including session start dates and mid-semester exams.
Semester Fee Schedule Notice: gndec.ac.in/sites/default/files/fnjanjun26.pdf
Detailed fee deadlines and fine structures specifically for the Jan–June 2026 period.

If you want GNDEC holidays or academic calendar details, the holiday list, semester academic schedule, and fee deadline notice are usually available separately. Holiday PDFs include gazetted and restricted holidays, while the academic calendar page shows session planning and exam-related dates.
